generatedFrom: claude v2.1.212 (fixtures; drift-verified on 2.1.214 — read-only surfaces)

systemInit.note: Init-fixture pin MUST be captured under the full AnyCode isolation flag set + isolated CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR (contract §4.3) — w0-01-persistence.jsonl was captured with --setting-sources project,local --strict-mcp-config but a DEFAULT config dir, so `mcp_servers`/`tools` sizes there are not AnyCode's own spawn shape; only the KEY-PATH structure (not counts) is pinned here.

resultSubtypes.localZeroCostDiscriminator: num_turns:0 AND duration_api_ms:0 (message.model:"<synthetic>" on the paired assistant frame)

initialize.note: account.subscriptionType is live only when SIGNED IN; account.tokenSource is live only on a SIGNED-OUT probe (w0-07/w0-13-authprobe-signedout) — the two are mutually exclusive, so neither is in requiredResponseKeyPaths (a universally-required set); the doctor/client read whichever is present. `commands[]` is deliberately NOT required: AnyCode's OWN spawn argv always carries --disable-slash-commands, which zeroes `commands` to `[]` BY DESIGN (probe #6) — the evidence fixture below (w0-13-authprobe-signedin.jsonl) omits that flag and does observe populated commands, but pinning a per-item field on it would contradict AnyCode's actual spawn contract; live-verified empty on the real 2.1.214 binary under AnyCode's own argv.

get_usage.note: CC-B B4 layer-2 live probe (2.1.214, this machine's own login) found a shape NOT in any W0 fixture: `rate_limits_available:false` with `subscription_type`/`rate_limits`/`behaviors` all `null` — plausibly a non-Claude-Max auth mode (API key / non-subscription). Only `rate_limits_available` itself and `session.total_cost_usd` are unconditionally required; the richer `limits[]` shape W0 captured is conditional on `rate_limits_available === true` and is NOT asserted by the always-on layer-1 gate (residual, quota-UI consumer CC-E must branch on the flag). Subset semantics is separately load-bearing here (contract §6.2): the live payload is already a strict superset of the SDK type (8 undeclared rate_limits buckets, all unreleased-feature codenames) — never validate with .strict()/exhaustive key enumeration.

get_context_usage.note: CC-B B4 layer-2 live probe (2.1.214) found `memoryFiles: []` on a run where `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` genuinely exists on disk — memoryFiles[] entry presence is not universal even for an existing global CLAUDE.md (mechanism not fully understood; plausibly tied to the same non-subscription auth mode that also zeroed get_usage's rate_limits, or to cwd not being a project the CLI recognizes). The per-entry shape (`path`/`type`/`tokens`) is still evidenced live whenever the array IS populated (all four w0-17 custody arms) — memoryFiles[].path carries the REAL home path even under full CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR isolation (R-W0-6/C2), and CC-B's client redacts it at the parse layer; the redactor is unit-tested directly (claude-client.test.ts) and via the w0-17-custody-B-isolated.jsonl regression, independent of whether THIS run's array happened to be empty.

set_permission_mode.note: Echo-body ack — differs from set_model, which has NO response body on success at all.

set_model.note: Success = {subtype:"success",request_id} with NO `response` key at all (absent, not empty). Reject = {subtype:"error",error:string}. Discriminate on response.subtype, never on the error text.

controlCancelRequest.note: CLI -> host, withdrawing one of ITS own pending control_request(s) to us (contract §2.2 pairing rule) — our client must drop the pending handler without answering.

unhandledSubtypeRule: Any control_request subtype CC-B does not implement MUST still get a fail-closed control_response error envelope carrying the same request_id — silence hangs the CLI's turn (contract §2.2, normative).
